task_completion.description: Lateral ventricles are segmented in the Segment Editor
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 3D Slicer interface (Segment Editor module), look for a colored mask (overlay) covering the dark, fluid-filled butterfly-shaped structures (ventricles) in the center of the brain on the axial (Red) slice view.

task_completion.description: Ventricular volume is calculated using Segment Statistics
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Segment Statistics' module panel is visible, displaying a table with a row for the ventricle segment and a column showing the volume (in mL, cc, or mm3).

task_completion.description: Rulers for Evans' Index are placed on the axial slice
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the slice views (specifically Axial/Red), two straight lines (markups) are visible: one measuring the maximum width of the frontal horns of the ventricles, and one measuring the maximum internal diameter of the skull.

task_completion.description: Final report and output files are saved and contain required data
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer, terminal, or text editor shows the file 'ventricle_report.json'. Ideally, the content is visible, showing fields for 'ventricular_volume_ml', 'evans_index', and a 'classification' (Normal, Mild, Moderate, or Severe).

integrity.description: Agent uses the 3D Slicer GUI modules
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking buttons and interacting with panels in the Segment Editor and Markups modules, rather than performing all tasks via a Python console script.

integrity.description: Report is derived from analysis, not pre-written
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not simply write the JSON report using a text editor or `echo` command without first performing the segmentation and measurement steps in the Slicer interface.

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the actual Slicer application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown is the actual 3D Slicer desktop application with 3D/2D views, not a mock-up or unrelated image viewer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The lateral ventricles are dark, fluid-filled spaces in the center of the brain. On an axial MRI slice, they resemble a butterfly shape. Evans' Index is the ratio of the maximum width of the frontal horns (front part of ventricles) to the maximum internal skull diameter on the same slice. Normal Evans' Index is < 0.30. Internal skull diameter is typically 130-170 mm. Task classification: Normal (Index < 0.30, Vol 7-20mL), Mild (Index 0.30-0.33), Moderate (Index 0.33-0.37), Severe (Index > 0.37).
